steps.title: Build line j from two points
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y=3x-2$, so its slope is $3$. Since line $j$ is parallel to line $k$, line $j$ also has slope $3$. The point $(0,1)$ is on line $j$; moving $1$ unit right raises $y$ by $3$, so another point is $(1,4)$.

steps.title: Fit line j in Desmos
steps.content: Enter $x_1=[0,1]$ and $y_1=[1,4]$, then run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=3$ and $b=1$, so line $j$ is $y=3x+1$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and test all choices
steps.content:
Line j passes through $(0, 1)$. Substituting: $1 = 3(0) + b \implies b = 1$. So line j is $y = 3x + 1$.
  • Choice A — $y = x - 2$: Slope = 1 $ eq 3$. Not parallel to line k. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 3x + 1$: Slope = 3 (parallel); passes through $(0,1)$ since $y = 3(0)+1 = 1$. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = -x - 2$: Slope = $-1 eq 3$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = -3x + 1$: Slope = $-3 eq 3$. Not parallel (perpendicular slope would be $- rac{1}{3}$). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
